title: Summit & Sable eCommerce catalog: batch color-grade six product shots to house look + recolor hardware/label accents without reshoot

source.reference: Professional Photoshop Editor Needed for Ongoing eCommerce Product Editing & Color Swapping

grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: "highly skilled and reliable Photoshop professional for ongoing eCommerce product photo manipulation"; "changing colors on specific product parts without having to reshoot the images"; example work "Changing the colors of text/design on product labels" and "Swapping colors on hardware (e.g., winch hooks, accessories) from existing photos"; The Catch: "You must be able to change these colors without ruining the original image. The lighting, shadows, and textures must remain completely natural. We cannot accept sloppy masking, flat colors, or unrealistic edits."
one_line_ask: Batch color-grade six existing Summit & Sable product photos to one consistent house look, then non-destructively recolor the recovery-strap hardware and label accents on two hero shots — keeping lighting, shadow and texture natural — and export web-ready catalog images.
full_brief: Summit & Sable is a direct-to-consumer overland and off-road recovery gear brand refreshing its online catalog for the new season. We are NOT reshooting — we need our existing product photos cleaned up and made consistent, plus a couple of color variants generated from shots we already have, exactly the way our old Photoshop editor used to do it. We're handing off six existing product photos (JPEG, roughly 2400x3000, shot in mixed lighting across two sessions so they don't match): a red recovery strap coiled on white sweep, the same strap's anodized aluminum snatch-hook shackle in close-up, a soft shackle, a folding shovel, a recovery tracks pair, and a branded ratchet strap with our printed label visible. Two jobs. First, batch grade: run all six through one consistent HOUSE LOOK so the catalog reads as a matched set — auto-tone the flat ones, correct the cool/warm white-balance mismatch between the two sessions to a neutral daylight, even out exposure, add a touch of vibrance so the products pop on a white PDP background, and lock a single Lightroom-style preset across the batch so contrast and tone are identical. Do not blow out the whites or crush the blacks; catalog images must stay true-to-product. Second, color variants WITHOUT a reshoot: on the recovery-strap hero, swap the strap-hardware accent from our current safety-orange anodizing to a gunmetal/graphite finish so we can list a second colorway; and on the ratchet-strap shot, shift the printed label's red brand block to our new forest-green house color. THE CATCH (this is what our last editor got wrong): change the color without ruining the shot — the lighting, shadows, metal reflections and woven-webbing texture must remain completely natural. No sloppy masking, no flat dead-fill color, no unrealistic edits; the recolor has to sit under the existing highlights and shadows. Deliverables: the six graded catalog JPEGs at web resolution (long edge 2000px, sRGB) and the two recolored variant images.

inputs.name: prod_01_recovery_strap_red.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a bright safety-orange/red coiled off-road recovery tow strap with a woven nylon webbing texture and a metal loop, arranged on a seamless white sweep background. Slightly cool/blue white balance, flat under-lit studio lighting, real fabric weave detail, subtle soft shadow under the coil, no reflections blown out. Catalog-style but slightly dull and un-graded, as shot straight off the camera.
inputs.realism_notes: Hero shot #1. Deliberately cool WB + flat lighting so the house grade has real work; the orange hardware/webbing is the accent that gets recolored to gunmetal. gpt-image-2 for photoreal product + fabric texture.

inputs.name: prod_02_snatch_shackle_closeup.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal macro ecommerce product photo of an anodized safety-orange aluminum off-road snatch shackle / recovery hook, close up on white seamless, showing real machined metal edges, specular highlights and a soft contact shadow. Slightly warm white balance and slightly overexposed compared to a matching set, real metal reflections, un-retouched catalog look.
inputs.realism_notes: Second-session shot with a DIFFERENT (warm, brighter) white balance than prod_01, so the batch genuinely needs WB matching to become a consistent set.

inputs.name: prod_03_soft_shackle.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a grey synthetic soft shackle rope loop for off-road recovery, on a white seamless background, flat even lighting, slightly muddy/low-contrast, neutral-cool cast, real braided-rope texture and a faint soft shadow. Straight-off-camera un-graded catalog shot.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat/low-contrast filler product to justify auto-tone + contrast preset across the batch.

inputs.name: prod_04_folding_shovel.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a black folding off-road recovery shovel / entrenching tool with an orange handle grip, laid on a white seamless background, dull grey-ish flat lighting, slightly underexposed, real metal and rubber texture, soft realistic shadow. Un-graded catalog shot.
inputs.realism_notes: Underexposed member of the set; exercises exposure lift + shared preset.

inputs.name: prod_05_recovery_tracks.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a pair of orange traction recovery boards / sand tracks stacked on a white seamless background, cool blueish white balance, flat lighting, real molded-plastic texture with cleats, soft shadow. Straight-off-camera catalog shot, un-retouched.
inputs.realism_notes: Another cool-WB member from session one; reinforces the need for consistent WB + vibrance.

inputs.name: prod_06_ratchet_strap_labeled.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal ecommerce product photo of a black ratchet tie-down strap coiled on a white seamless background, with a printed woven brand label sewn on the webbing showing a solid RED rectangular brand block. The label reads the brand name 'SUMMIT & SABLE' in small clean white text, with a smaller line of white spec text beneath it. Slightly warm white balance, flat lighting, real webbing weave and stitched-label texture, soft shadow. Un-graded catalog shot.
inputs.realism_notes: Second hero: carries the RED printed 'SUMMIT & SABLE' brand label block that gets hue-shifted to forest green. Warm WB again to justify batch matching. Label kept clean/legible with the canonical brand name so selection-by-prompt can isolate it.

inputs.name: summit_sable_house_look.xmp
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED asset (supplied by the client, not model-generated): a Lightroom develop preset (.xmp) named 'Summit & Sable House Look' capturing the brand's catalog tone — neutral-daylight white balance, gentle contrast S-curve, modest clarity, +10 vibrance, whites held below clipping for true-to-product PDP shots. Provided so the same look can be locked across the whole batch.
inputs.realism_notes: Supplied brand asset so image_apply_preset has a real named preset to lock across all six; mirrors how a catalog team enforces one consistent look. Not connector-generated.

outputs.name: catalog_graded_x6
outputs.spec: Six color-graded product JPEGs, auto-toned + white-balance-matched to neutral daylight + exposure-evened + vibrance-lifted + the Summit & Sable House Look preset applied uniformly; sRGB, long edge 2000px, true-to-product (no blown whites / crushed blacks), a consistent matched catalog set.

outputs.name: variant_strap_gunmetal.jpg
outputs.spec: Recolored variant of prod_01: the safety-orange strap hardware/accent recolored to a gunmetal/graphite finish via a masked hue-shift + luminosity-preserving overlay riding the existing luminosity, so highlights, shadows and woven-webbing texture stay natural; already graded to the house look; resized to the same web-resolution frame as the catalog set (sRGB, long edge 2000px).

outputs.name: variant_label_forest_green.jpg
outputs.spec: Recolored variant of prod_06: the printed label's red brand block hue-shifted to the new forest-green house color inside a tight selection while preserving the label's stitching texture, small white text legibility, and the surrounding webbing; already graded to the house look; resized to the same web-resolution frame as the catalog set (sRGB, long edge 2000px).

connector_workflow.inputs_from: prod_01_recovery_strap_red.jpg, prod_02_snatch_shackle_closeup.jpg, prod_03_soft_shackle.jpg, prod_04_folding_shovel.jpg, prod_05_recovery_tracks.jpg, prod_06_ratchet_strap_labeled.jpg, summit_sable_house_look.xmp
connector_workflow.note: Upload the six existing product photos and the House Look .xmp preset into the working project so the batch grade can run against real asset references.
Auto-tone prod_01 (cool, flat) to a sane baseline before white-balance and preset.
Warm prod_01's cool/blue cast toward neutral daylight so it matches the warm second-session shots.
Lift vibrance so the strap reads richly against a white PDP without oversaturating.
Lock the supplied Summit & Sable House Look .xmp (referenced from catalog_assets_uploaded) onto prod_01 to fix the shared contrast/tone (same preset reused across all six).
Auto-tone prod_02, the slightly overexposed warm shackle close-up.
Cool prod_02's warm cast toward the same neutral-daylight target so session one and two match.
Pull prod_02's exposure down slightly so its brightness matches the rest of the set (protect metal highlights).
Apply the House Look preset to prod_02 for identical tone.
Auto-tone the muddy low-contrast soft-shackle shot (prod_03).
Apply the House Look preset to prod_03.
Lift exposure on the underexposed folding-shovel shot (prod_04) toward the set brightness.
Auto-tone prod_04 after the exposure lift to normalize contrast.
Apply the House Look preset to prod_04.
Warm prod_05's cool cast (recovery tracks, session one) to the neutral-daylight target.
Apply the House Look preset to prod_05.
Auto-tone the warm, flat ratchet-strap shot (prod_06).
Cool prod_06's warm cast to match the neutral-daylight set.
Apply the House Look preset to prod_06 so all six now match as a catalog set.

On the graded strap hero, select just the orange hardware/accent ('orange metal loop and anodized fitting on the recovery strap') so the recolor is isolated to the part — no sloppy whole-image repaint. Returns a maskURI.

Within the selection (consumes the graded hero + the mask from step 20), drop the orange's saturation and shift hue toward neutral gunmetal, letting existing luminosity carry the metal reflections/shadows so it looks like a real graphite finish, not flat fill.

Blend a subtle graphite color overlay confined to the selected hardware (consumes the hue-shifted frame + the same mask; luminosity-preserving) to finish the gunmetal colorway while keeping woven-webbing texture and highlights natural. Still at the full ~2400x3000 hero frame; resized to web spec in step 23.
Resize the finished gunmetal recolor to the same web catalog spec as the catalog set (long edge 2000px, sRGB) so the variant matches its stated output frame.
On the graded ratchet-strap shot, select only the printed label's red brand block ('red rectangular printed label block') to isolate the hue change from the white text and webbing. Returns a maskURI.

Shift the selected red block's hue to the forest-green house color via HSL (consumes the graded shot + the mask from step 24), preserving the label's stitch texture and the small white text legibility (no flat overpaint). Still at the full hero frame; resized to web spec in step 26.
Resize the finished forest-green label recolor to the same web catalog spec as the catalog set (long edge 2000px, sRGB) so the variant matches its stated output frame.
Resize graded prod_01 to web catalog spec (long edge 2000px, sRGB).
Resize graded prod_02 to the same web catalog spec.
Resize graded prod_03 to web catalog spec.
Resize graded prod_04 to web catalog spec.
Resize graded prod_05 to web catalog spec.
Resize graded prod_06 to web catalog spec; web_p1..web_p6 form the catalog_graded_x6 deliverable.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chaining throughout, matching the established masked-edit pattern (select_by_prompt then an adjust step consuming base-image+mask). Six parallel grade sub-chains fan out from catalog_assets_uploaded(1); two recolor branches consume graded heroes and each end in a web-frame resize; six catalog resize steps produce the matched web set (web_p1..web_p6) while the two resized variants complete both colorways.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 32 connector calls, 10 distinct Adobe tools, per-image grade pipelines of differing depth converging on one house look, plus two isolated luminosity-preserving part recolors each resized to the web catalog frame. No banned capability used.

reverify.banned_capability_check: None of the banned capabilities are used. image_crop_and_resize is used only to downscale finished frames to the web catalog spec (long edge 2000px), never to extend/pad the canvas; no generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo; no upscaling/super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction/inpainting; no bespoke font design; no unreachable external account. Every pixel is graded or recolored from the six supplied originals plus the supplied .xmp preset.
reverify.notes: Corrections applied on reverify: (1) the two masked recolor adjust steps were rewired to the established masked-edit pattern (AO-32 steps 8/16/23) so each adjust consumes BOTH the graded base image AND the select_by_prompt mask -- step 21 image_adjust_hsl takes [p1_graded, strap_hardware_mask]; step 22 image_apply_color_overlay takes [strap_hue_shifted, strap_hardware_mask]; step 25 image_adjust_hsl takes [p6_graded, label_red_block_mask]; chaining_note synced accordingly. (2) The two recolored variants are now each downscaled to the web catalog frame (long edge 2000px, sRGB) via image_crop_and_resize -- step 23 resizes the gunmetal strap (variant_strap_gunmetal.jpg) and step 26 resizes the forest-green label (variant_label_forest_green.jpg) -- so both variant output specs are actually satisfied by the workflow rather than shipped at the raw ~2400x3000 hero size; the color-overlay/HSL recolor steps now emit intermediate full-res frames (strap_gunmetal_fullres, label_forest_green_fullres) that feed those resizes. Risky-looking steps stay in-bounds: the two color changes use image_select_by_prompt to isolate a real product part, then image_adjust_hsl (hue/sat shift on existing pixels) and image_apply_color_overlay (luminosity-preserving overlay) -- the palette's intended recolor path, NOT image_fill_area (solid dead-fill, deliberately unused) and NOT generative regeneration; existing luminosity/shadow/texture is retained, satisfying the brief's 'must remain completely natural' catch. image_apply_preset locks a client-SUPPLIED .xmp (declared authored input, not generated). No image_remove_background / compositing / background replacement -- recolors happen in place. All steps server=='adobe'. 32 calls (>=10), 10 distinct Adobe tools (>=6); qualifies as long-horizon flagship for a color_grade_lr / edit task. Slot-match confirmed: category=color_grade_lr (fits slot keywords product photo/color grade/batch/ecommerce/edit), operation O1 Photo & Image, output_modality=image, workflow_nature=edit, vertical=retail_ecommerce; IP-clean fictional brand Summit & Sable.
